seoTitle: How to Fill a Shape With Color in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to fill a shape with color in Canva, including solid colors, gradients, and image fills for more dynamic designs.
intro: Filling shapes with color is one of the most basic but essential Canva skills, letting you build backgrounds, highlight areas, and add visual structure to any design. This guide covers the different fill options available.

steps.heading: Add a shape to your canvas
steps.body: Search Elements for a basic shape like a rectangle, circle, or custom icon, and drag it into your design.

steps.heading: Select the shape
steps.body: Click on the shape to bring up its color and styling options in the toolbar.

steps.heading: Choose a fill color
steps.body: Click the color swatch and pick from your brand colors, Canva's palette, or enter a custom hex code.

steps.heading: Try a gradient or image fill
steps.body: For more visual interest, apply a gradient fill or drag an image into the shape to fill it with a photo.

steps.heading: Adjust opacity if needed
steps.body: Lower the shape's opacity if you want the fill color to blend more subtly with the background.

faq.question: Can I use my exact brand color when filling a shape?
faq.answer: Yes, you can enter a custom hex code to match your brand's exact color.

faq.question: Can I fill a shape with an image instead of a color?
faq.answer: Yes, dragging a photo onto a shape will fill it, similar to how frames work.

faq.question: Is a gradient fill available for free?
faq.answer: Basic gradient fills are available on Canva's free plan.

faq.question: Can I save a custom color for reuse later?
faq.answer: Yes, custom colors you add are saved to your recent or brand color palette for future use.
